title: rosa multiflora var. mokanensis
intro: Rosa multiflora var. mokanensis, also known as Korean rose or wild rose, is a variety of the multiflora rose species native to East Asia. It is a perennial shrub with fragrant flowers and edible hips, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties. The hips of Rosa multiflora var. mokanensis contain high levels of vitamin C, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, contributing to various health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health, enhanced immune function, and reduced risk of certain cancers. Rosa multiflora var. mokanensis can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its hips can be used in herbal remedies, jams, and preserves.
